# ecdsa

```go
import "crypto/ecdsa"
```

Package ecdsa implements the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm, as defined in [FIPS 186\\\-5](<https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.186-5.pdf>).

Signatures generated by this package are not deterministic, but entropy is mixed with the private key and the message, achieving the same level of security in case of randomness source failure.

Operations involving private keys are implemented using constant\-time algorithms, as long as an \[elliptic.Curve\] returned by \[elliptic.P224\], \[elliptic.P256\], \[elliptic.P384\], or \[elliptic.P521\] is used.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/ecdsa"
	"crypto/elliptic"
	"crypto/rand"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	privateKey, err := ecdsa.GenerateKey(elliptic.P256(), rand.Reader)
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}

	msg := "hello, world"
	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(msg))

	sig, err := ecdsa.SignASN1(rand.Reader, privateKey, hash[:])
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
	fmt.Printf("signature: %x\n", sig)

	valid := ecdsa.VerifyASN1(&privateKey.PublicKey, hash[:], sig)
	fmt.Println("signature verified:", valid)
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Sign"></a>

## func [Sign](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa_legacy.go#L69>)

```go
func Sign(rand io.Reader, priv *PrivateKey, hash []byte) (r, s *big.Int, err error)
```

Sign signs a hash \(which should be the result of hashing a larger message\) using the private key, priv. If the hash is longer than the bit\-length of the private key's curve order, the hash will be truncated to that length. It returns the signature as a pair of integers. Most applications should use [SignASN1](<#SignASN1>) instead of dealing directly with r, s.

The signature is randomized. Since Go 1.26, a secure source of random bytes is always used, and the Reader is ignored unless GODEBUG=cryptocustomrand=1 is set. This setting will be removed in a future Go release. Instead, use [testing/cryptotest.SetGlobalRandom](<https://pkg.go.dev/testing/cryptotest/#SetGlobalRandom>).

<a name="SignASN1"></a>

## func [SignASN1](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L382>)

```go
func SignASN1(r io.Reader, priv *PrivateKey, hash []byte) ([]byte, error)
```

SignASN1 signs a hash \(which should be the result of hashing a larger message\) using the private key, priv. If the hash is longer than the bit\-length of the private key's curve order, the hash will be truncated to that length. It returns the ASN.1 encoded signature.

The signature is randomized. Since Go 1.26, a secure source of random bytes is always used, and the Reader is ignored unless GODEBUG=cryptocustomrand=1 is set. This setting will be removed in a future Go release. Instead, use [testing/cryptotest.SetGlobalRandom](<https://pkg.go.dev/testing/cryptotest/#SetGlobalRandom>).

<a name="Verify"></a>

## func [Verify](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa_legacy.go#L149>)

```go
func Verify(pub *PublicKey, hash []byte, r, s *big.Int) bool
```

Verify verifies the signature in r, s of hash using the public key, pub. Its return value records whether the signature is valid. Most applications should use VerifyASN1 instead of dealing directly with r, s.

The inputs are not considered confidential, and may leak through timing side channels, or if an attacker has control of part of the inputs.

<a name="VerifyASN1"></a>

## func [VerifyASN1](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L496>)

```go
func VerifyASN1(pub *PublicKey, hash, sig []byte) bool
```

VerifyASN1 verifies the ASN.1 encoded signature, sig, of hash using the public key, pub. Its return value records whether the signature is valid.

The inputs are not considered confidential, and may leak through timing side channels, or if an attacker has control of part of the inputs.

<a name="PrivateKey"></a>

## type [PrivateKey](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L168-L180>)

PrivateKey represents an ECDSA private key.

```go
type PrivateKey struct {
    PublicKey

    // D is the private scalar value.
    //
    // Deprecated: modifying the raw value can produce invalid keys, and may
    // invalidate internal optimizations; moreover, [big.Int] methods are not
    // suitable for operating on cryptographic values. To encode and decode
    // PrivateKey values, use [PrivateKey.Bytes] and [ParseRawPrivateKey] or
    // [crypto/x509.MarshalPKCS8PrivateKey] and [crypto/x509.ParsePKCS8PrivateKey].
    // For ECDH, use [crypto/ecdh].
    D   *big.Int
}
```

<a name="GenerateKey"></a>

### func [GenerateKey](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L336>)

```go
func GenerateKey(c elliptic.Curve, r io.Reader) (*PrivateKey, error)
```

GenerateKey generates a new ECDSA private key for the specified curve.

Since Go 1.26, a secure source of random bytes is always used, and the Reader is ignored unless GODEBUG=cryptocustomrand=1 is set. This setting will be removed in a future Go release. Instead, use [testing/cryptotest.SetGlobalRandom](<https://pkg.go.dev/testing/cryptotest/#SetGlobalRandom>).

<a name="ParseRawPrivateKey"></a>

### func [ParseRawPrivateKey](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L246>)

```go
func ParseRawPrivateKey(curve elliptic.Curve, data []byte) (*PrivateKey, error)
```

ParseRawPrivateKey parses a private key encoded as a fixed\-length big\-endian integer, according to SEC 1, Version 2.0, Section 2.3.6 \(sometimes referred to as the raw format\). It returns an error if the value is not reduced modulo the curve's order, or if it's zero.

curve must be one of \[elliptic.P224\], \[elliptic.P256\], \[elliptic.P384\], or \[elliptic.P521\], or ParseRawPrivateKey returns an error.

ParseRawPrivateKey accepts the same format as \[ecdh.Curve.NewPrivateKey\] does for NIST curves, but returns a [PrivateKey](<#PrivateKey>) instead of an \[ecdh.PrivateKey\].

Note that private keys are more commonly encoded in ASN.1 or PKCS\#8 format, which can be parsed with [crypto/x509.ParseECPrivateKey](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509/#ParseECPrivateKey>) or [crypto/x509.ParsePKCS8PrivateKey](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509/#ParsePKCS8PrivateKey>) \(and [encoding/pem](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/pem/>)\).

<a name="PrivateKey.Bytes"></a>

### func \(\*PrivateKey\) [Bytes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L285>)

```go
func (priv *PrivateKey) Bytes() ([]byte, error)
```

Bytes encodes the private key as a fixed\-length big\-endian integer according to SEC 1, Version 2.0, Section 2.3.6 \(sometimes referred to as the raw format\). It returns an error if the private key is invalid.

PrivateKey.Curve must be one of \[elliptic.P224\], \[elliptic.P256\], \[elliptic.P384\], or \[elliptic.P521\], or Bytes returns an error.

Bytes returns the same format as \[ecdh.PrivateKey.Bytes\] does for NIST curves.

Note that private keys are more commonly encoded in ASN.1 or PKCS\#8 format, which can be generated with [crypto/x509.MarshalECPrivateKey](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509/#MarshalECPrivateKey>) or [crypto/x509.MarshalPKCS8PrivateKey](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509/#MarshalPKCS8PrivateKey>) \(and [encoding/pem](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/pem/>)\).

<a name="PrivateKey.ECDH"></a>

### func \(\*PrivateKey\) [ECDH](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L185>)

```go
func (priv *PrivateKey) ECDH() (*ecdh.PrivateKey, error)
```

ECDH returns k as a \[ecdh.PrivateKey\]. It returns an error if the key is invalid according to the definition of \[ecdh.Curve.NewPrivateKey\], or if the Curve is not supported by [crypto/ecdh](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/ecdh/>).

<a name="PrivateKey.Equal"></a>

### func \(\*PrivateKey\) [Equal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L218>)

```go
func (priv *PrivateKey) Equal(x crypto.PrivateKey) bool
```

Equal reports whether priv and x have the same value.

See [PublicKey.Equal](<#PublicKey.Equal>) for details on how Curve is compared.

<a name="PrivateKey.Public"></a>

### func \(\*PrivateKey\) [Public](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L211>)

```go
func (priv *PrivateKey) Public() crypto.PublicKey
```

Public returns the public key corresponding to priv.

<a name="PrivateKey.Sign"></a>

### func \(\*PrivateKey\) [Sign](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L323>)

```go
func (priv *PrivateKey) Sign(random io.Reader, digest []byte, opts crypto.SignerOpts) ([]byte, error)
```

Sign signs a hash \(which should be the result of hashing a larger message with opts.HashFunc\(\)\) using the private key, priv. If the hash is longer than the bit\-length of the private key's curve order, the hash will be truncated to that length. It returns the ASN.1 encoded signature, like [SignASN1](<#SignASN1>).

If random is not nil, the signature is randomized. Most applications should use [crypto/rand.Reader](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/rand/#Reader>) as random, but unless GODEBUG=cryptocustomrand=1 is set, a secure source of random bytes is always used, and the actual Reader is ignored. The GODEBUG setting will be removed in a future Go release. Instead, use [testing/cryptotest.SetGlobalRandom](<https://pkg.go.dev/testing/cryptotest/#SetGlobalRandom>).

If random is nil, Sign will produce a deterministic signature according to RFC 6979. When producing a deterministic signature, opts.HashFunc\(\) must be the function used to produce digest and priv.Curve must be one of \[elliptic.P224\], \[elliptic.P256\], \[elliptic.P384\], or \[elliptic.P521\].

<a name="PublicKey"></a>

## type [PublicKey](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L42-L55>)

PublicKey represents an ECDSA public key.

```go
type PublicKey struct {
    elliptic.Curve

    // X, Y are the coordinates of the public key point.
    //
    // Deprecated: modifying the raw coordinates can produce invalid keys, and may
    // invalidate internal optimizations; moreover, [big.Int] methods are not
    // suitable for operating on cryptographic values. To encode and decode
    // PublicKey values, use [PublicKey.Bytes] and [ParseUncompressedPublicKey]
    // or [crypto/x509.MarshalPKIXPublicKey] and [crypto/x509.ParsePKIXPublicKey].
    // For ECDH, use [crypto/ecdh]. For lower-level elliptic curve operations,
    // use a third-party module like filippo.io/nistec.
    X, Y *big.Int
}
```

<a name="ParseUncompressedPublicKey"></a>

### func [ParseUncompressedPublicKey](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L107>)

```go
func ParseUncompressedPublicKey(curve elliptic.Curve, data []byte) (*PublicKey, error)
```

ParseUncompressedPublicKey parses a public key encoded as an uncompressed point according to SEC 1, Version 2.0, Section 2.3.3 \(also known as the X9.62 uncompressed format\). It returns an error if the point is not in uncompressed form, is not on the curve, or is the point at infinity.

curve must be one of \[elliptic.P224\], \[elliptic.P256\], \[elliptic.P384\], or \[elliptic.P521\], or ParseUncompressedPublicKey returns an error.

ParseUncompressedPublicKey accepts the same format as \[ecdh.Curve.NewPublicKey\] does for NIST curves, but returns a [PublicKey](<#PublicKey>) instead of an \[ecdh.PublicKey\].

Note that public keys are more commonly encoded in DER \(or PEM\) format, which can be parsed with [crypto/x509.ParsePKIXPublicKey](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509/#ParsePKIXPublicKey>) \(and [encoding/pem](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/pem/>)\).

<a name="PublicKey.Bytes"></a>

### func \(\*PublicKey\) [Bytes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L144>)

```go
func (pub *PublicKey) Bytes() ([]byte, error)
```

Bytes encodes the public key as an uncompressed point according to SEC 1, Version 2.0, Section 2.3.3 \(also known as the X9.62 uncompressed format\). It returns an error if the public key is invalid.

PublicKey.Curve must be one of \[elliptic.P224\], \[elliptic.P256\], \[elliptic.P384\], or \[elliptic.P521\], or Bytes returns an error.

Bytes returns the same format as \[ecdh.PublicKey.Bytes\] does for NIST curves.

Note that public keys are more commonly encoded in DER \(or PEM\) format, which can be generated with [crypto/x509.MarshalPKIXPublicKey](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/x509/#MarshalPKIXPublicKey>) \(and [encoding/pem](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/pem/>)\).

<a name="PublicKey.ECDH"></a>

### func \(\*PublicKey\) [ECDH](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L63>)

```go
func (pub *PublicKey) ECDH() (*ecdh.PublicKey, error)
```

ECDH returns k as a \[ecdh.PublicKey\]. It returns an error if the key is invalid according to the definition of \[ecdh.Curve.NewPublicKey\], or if the Curve is not supported by crypto/ecdh.

<a name="PublicKey.Equal"></a>

### func \(\*PublicKey\) [Equal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/ecdsa/ecdsa.go#L80>)

```go
func (pub *PublicKey) Equal(x crypto.PublicKey) bool
```

Equal reports whether pub and x have the same value.

Two keys are only considered to have the same value if they have the same Curve value. Note that for example \[elliptic.P256\] and elliptic.P256\(\).Params\(\) are different values, as the latter is a generic not constant time implementation.
